## 1. About Vlas Fedorov

**Name:** Vlas Fedorov
**Profession:** Performance Marketing Specialist
**Experience:** 10+ years in digital marketing
**Location:** Russia (works internationally)
**Languages:** Russian, English
**Website:** https://vlasdobry.ru

### Expertise
- Performance Marketing (paid traffic management)
- Growth Analytics (end-to-end analytics)
- Traffic scaling while maintaining unit economics
- LTV/CAC optimization
- Yandex Direct, Google Ads
- Sales funnels and CRM strategies
- SEO and GEO (Generative Engine Optimization)

### Key Results
- 10x sales growth in 6 months (record)
- $110K ad budget managed per month
- CPA 5x below target ($1.05 vs $5 goal)
- ROMI x2.39, revenue x2
- 50+ SEO audits since 2020
- 20+ GEO audits

### Methodology
SOSTAC + PDCA: audit -> strategy -> launch -> control

## 2. Services

### SEO Audit
Complete website analysis with ready-to-use code.

**What I Check (7 Sections):**
- Technical SEO: robots.txt, sitemap.xml, HTTPS, canonical, indexing, Core Web Vitals
- On-Page SEO: Title, Description, H1-H6 headers, URL structure, internal linking
- Content: Image alt tags, content quality and depth, factual density
- Schema.org: Structured markup for rich snippets (LocalBusiness, Hotel, MedicalBusiness, Product, FAQ)
- Local SEO: Google Maps, Yandex Maps, NAP consistency, reviews
- Reputation: Review analysis, brand mentions, presence in directories and aggregators
- E-E-A-T: Expertise, authoritativeness, trust signals

**Deliverables:**
- 10-15 page PDF report with scores
- Issue tables with priorities
- Ready-to-use code (robots.txt, Schema.org, meta tags)
- Weekly action plan

**Pricing:**
- Health Score: Free (8 auto-checks)
- Full Audit: $150 (15,000 RUB)
- Audit + Implementation: $300 (30,000 RUB)

**Page:** https://vlasdobry.ru/services/seo/

### GEO Audit (Generative Engine Optimization)
Optimization for AI search engines: ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude, Gemini, YandexGPT, GigaChat.

**What is GEO?**
GEO (Generative Engine Optimization) is optimization for AI search engines. People used to search on Google. Now they ask ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude. AI doesn't show 10 links — it gives 2-5 specific recommendations. If you're not in those recommendations — you're losing clients.

**What I Check:**
- AI Visibility: Testing in 6 AI systems
- LLM Files: llms.txt and llms-full.txt
- Structured Data: Schema.org markup
- FAQ / Q&A: Structured answers for AI to cite
- E-E-A-T Signals: Authorship, contacts, social links
- Factual Density: Concrete numbers vs marketing fluff
- Reputation: Ratings and reviews

**Deliverables:**
- PDF report with scores
- AI visibility testing screenshots
- Competitor analysis
- Ready-to-use code (llms.txt, llms-full.txt, Schema.org, FAQPage)
- Weekly action plan
- Monitoring toolkit for 3 months

**Pricing:**
- Health Score: Free (5 auto-checks)
- Full GEO Audit: from $750 (50,000 RUB)

**Page:** https://vlasdobry.ru/services/geo/

### PPC Advertising for Hotels
Pay-per-click advertising management for hotels and hospitality.

**What I Do:**
- Campaign Audit: structure, bidding, negative keywords, placements — finding wasted budget and growth opportunities
- Keyword Research: collection, clustering, competitor analysis, traffic and budget forecasting
- Campaign Setup: Yandex Direct, Google Ads, Performance Max, retargeting, dynamic ads
- Analytics: end-to-end attribution from click to booking to revenue (Yandex Metrika, Google Analytics, Roistat)
- A/B Testing: ads, landing pages, bidding strategies
- Optimization: bid management, ROAS targeting, CPA optimization, placement exclusions
- Reporting: weekly reports in bookings and revenue, not clicks and CTR

**Results:**
- Grace Group (hotel chain, 20 properties): revenue +60% in low season, conversion x2 (6.8% to 13.8%), ad cost ratio on target
- Odyssey Flower (SPA resorts): conversions x10, revenue x4, ad cost ratio from 48% to 13%

**Deliverables:**
- Audit of current ad campaigns with budget breakdown
- Media plan with traffic, booking, and ROAS forecast
- Configured campaigns in Yandex Direct and/or Google Ads
- End-to-end analytics: ads to website to booking to revenue
- Weekly reports in bookings and revenue
- Ongoing optimization of bids, placements, and ads

**Website Readiness Checklist for Paid Ads (Free):**
1. Online booking engine is working (Booking.com widget, SynXis, SiteMinder)
2. Website loads fast (under 3 seconds on mobile)
3. Mobile version is responsive and user-friendly
4. Rates and availability on website are up to date
5. Contact details and address are prominently displayed
6. Room photos are professional and current
7. Google Analytics / tracking is connected
8. Conversion goals are set up (bookings, calls, forms)

Check your website free: https://vlasdobry.ru/en/services/seo/ (SEO Health Score)

**Pricing:**
- Checklist: Free (website readiness for paid ads)
- Account Audit: from 10,000 RUB ($150)
- Management: from 50,000 RUB/mo ($1,000/mo)

**Page:** https://vlasdobry.ru/services/ppc/

### 168-FZ Compliance Checker
Free tool to check if a Russian website complies with Federal Law 168-FZ on state language protection (effective March 1, 2026).

**What It Checks:**
- Buttons and CTAs in foreign languages (e.g. "Book Now", "Check In", "Submit")
- Headings (h1-h6) with Latin text
- Navigation menus in foreign languages
- Form labels and placeholders
- Meta elements (title, description)

**How It Works:**
- Parses visible UI elements using Cloudflare HTMLRewriter (streaming, no full page download)
- Detects Latin script (2+ consecutive letters) in consumer-facing elements
- Whitelist for technical terms (URL, HTTP, PDF, Wi-Fi) and patterns (emails, domains)
- Severity scoring: critical (-10), important (-7), medium (-5), info (0)
- SPA detection: shows "Limited analysis" for JavaScript-rendered sites

**Scoring:**
- 80-100: Compliant (green)
- 40-79: Warnings (yellow)
- 0-39: Non-compliant (red)

**Free:** Unlimited checks, no registration.

**Page:** https://vlasdobry.ru/168-fz/

## 3. Industry Expertise

### Hotels (HoReCa)
**Focus:** More direct bookings, less OTA dependency. In-house at Grace Group — international hotel chain, 20 properties.

**Services for Hotels:**
- Paid traffic for direct bookings: Google Ads, Google Hotel Ads, metasearch — channel management focused on cost per booking and ROI
- Direct booking funnels: retargeting, abandoned booking recovery, email sequences for guest retention
- Business-language analytics: connecting ad platforms with PMS and booking engine. Reports in bookings and revenue, not clicks.

**Pricing:** Audit 50-80K RUB ($800-$1,500), ongoing management from 80K RUB/mo ($1,500/mo). Min ad budget: 80K RUB/mo ($1,500/mo).
**Results:** +60% revenue in low season, 2x paid traffic conversion.

**Page:** https://vlasdobry.ru/for-hotels/

### SPA & Wellness
**Focus:** Fill capacity, reduce acquisition cost.

**Services for SPA:**
- Strategy & Analytics
- Search & Display Advertising with geo-targeting
- Influencers & SMM
- Lead generation funnels
- Team management

**Case Result:** Conversions x10, ad cost ratio 48% -> 13%, brand queries x5
**Entry point:** Free 20-minute marketing review

**Page:** https://vlasdobry.ru/for-spa/

### DNA Laboratories (MedTech)
**Focus:** Scalable lead growth without sacrificing ROI.

**Services for Labs:**
- Search advertising (Google Ads, Yandex Direct)
- Geo-targeted ads for collection points
- Finding new traffic channels
- Analytics and dashboards by location
- Scaling to new markets

**Case Result:** CPA $1.05 (target $5), 8,700+ conversions, #1 Google Ads auction share
**Min ad budget:** 50K RUB/mo ($1,000/mo)

**Page:** https://vlasdobry.ru/for-labs/

## 4. Case Studies

### Grace Group — International Hotel Chain
20 properties, in-house performance marketing.
- Challenge: Systematic guest acquisition through direct channels, reducing OTA dependency
- Results: +60% revenue in low season, 2x paid traffic conversion
- Tags: Hospitality, In-house, Performance, Analytics

### Odyssey Flower
SPA resort chain on Black Sea coast.
- Challenge: High acquisition cost (48% ad ratio), low occupancy
- Results: Conversions x10, ad cost 48% -> 13%, brand queries x5
- Tags: Wellness, Performance, Geo-targeting, Analytics

### DNA Lab Network
5 countries: Russia, Georgia, Kazakhstan, Uzbekistan, Azerbaijan.
- Challenge: Build client acquisition across countries without increasing CPA
- Results: CPA $1.05 (target $5), 8,700+ conversions, #1 auction share
- Tags: MedTech, International, Performance, Analytics

### All Right
Online English school for kids, Delaware, USA.
- Results: +167% sales, $110K budget, ads in 15+ languages
- Tags: EdTech, International, B2C

### Kiwitaxi
Transfer booking platform, 102 countries.
- Results: ROMI x2.39, revenue x2, 400+ campaigns in 5 languages
- Tags: Travel, Performance, Scale

SaaS for optimizing business visibility in generative AI systems.
- Tech: Next.js, FastAPI, Celery, PostgreSQL

Personal finance web app with budgets and spending analytics.
- Tech: React, Node.js, PostgreSQL
- Status: Live

Online wish-making platform with cosmic visual experience.
- Tech: Next.js, Framer Motion, GSAP
- Status: Live
- URL: https://onlinezhelaniya.ru/

### Metabolism Marathon Bot
Telegram bot for nutrition company with sales funnels and AI questionnaires.
- Tech: Python, PostgreSQL, Docker, Telegram API
- Status: Live

### Vocalis.Expert
Acoustic voice analysis and training platform.
- Tech: Next.js, Web Audio API, Three.js, WebRTC
- Status: In Development

## 6. Blog Articles

### Hotel Brand Traffic: The Cost of Missing Brand Ads
**URL:** /blog/brendovyj-trafik-otelya-keys/
**Category:** MARKETING
**Date:** 2026-03-10

Case study of a resort hotel (58 rooms): 1,300 branded searches/month, 0 search ads, while competitors run 23 ads on the hotel's brand name. Potential losses of $750-1,000/month from competitor poaching and OTA commissions. Google visibility dropped 61% in 16 months. Brand protection campaign costs $100-200/month with 3-8× ROI.
### Russia's Language Law 168-FZ: What to Change on Your Website Before March 2026
**URL:** /blog/168-fz-zakon-o-russkom-yazyke-sayt/
**Category:** MARKETING
**Date:** 2026-02-25

Practical compliance guide for Russia's Federal Law 168-FZ on state language protection, effective March 1, 2026. The law requires all consumer-facing business information to be in Russian — including websites. Contains a checklist of website elements to localize (buttons, room categories, booking forms), fine amounts (up to 500,000 RUB / ~$5,000 for advertising violations), exceptions (registered trademarks, words in normative dictionaries), and SEO benefits of Russian-language content for Yandex rankings.
### GEO Optimization for Your Website: A Step-by-Step Guide (2026)
**URL:** /blog/geo-optimizaciya-sajta-gajd/
**Category:** GEO
**Date:** 2026-02-22

Practical guide to GEO optimization: llms.txt, Schema.org, FAQ, E-E-A-T signals. A 5-step checklist with code examples.

Key insight: GEO optimization comes down to 5 concrete steps: create llms.txt, add Schema.org markup, write AI-friendly FAQ, strengthen E-E-A-T signals, and ensure AI bot access. Below is a step-by-step checklist with code examples.

FAQ:
- Can I do GEO optimization myself? Yes, the basic steps (llms.txt, robots.txt, FAQ) can be done in 1-2 days. Schema.org and deep optimization may require a developer.
- Does GEO optimization work for all industries? It works best for local businesses (hotels, clinics, restaurants) and professional services (lawyers, marketers, developers). For e-commerce with thousands of products, the approach differs.
- How do I check if my site appears in ChatGPT responses? Ask ChatGPT a question about your niche in your area. For example: "Best hotel in downtown LA?" or "Where to get an SEO audit?" If you're not in the answer, it's time to start GEO optimization.

### GEO Promotion: Why Your Business Needs an AI Traffic Channel
**URL:** /blog/geo-prodvizhenie-sajta/
**Category:** GEO
**Date:** 2026-02-22

What GEO promotion delivers for businesses. ROI, comparison with paid ads, and the funnel from AI query to customer.

Key insight: GEO promotion is a strategy for attracting customers through AI search engines (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude). Unlike paid ads, there's no cost per click. Optimize once — get recommended for free.

FAQ:
- How is GEO promotion different from SEO? SEO targets rankings in Google and Bing. GEO targets mentions in ChatGPT, Perplexity, and other AI systems. [Detailed comparison](/en/blog/chto-takoe-geo-optimizaciya/) is in a separate article. Using both channels together is optimal.
- Can I hire someone for GEO promotion? Yes. A free diagnostic shows your current state. Full audit with recommendations and implementation is available on the [service page](/en/services/geo/). Basic steps can be done independently using the [guide](/en/blog/geo-optimizaciya-sajta-gajd/).
- How long until I see results? Technical changes (llms.txt, Schema.org) get indexed in 1-2 weeks. First mentions in AI answers appear within 2-4 weeks. Consistent flow takes 1-2 months with full optimization.

### llms.txt: What It Is and Why Your Website Needs One
**URL:** /blog/llms-txt-chto-eto/
**Category:** GEO
**Date:** 2026-02-22

What is llms.txt, how to create it, and where to place it. Examples for different industries, specification, common mistakes.

Key insight: llms.txt is a text file at your site root that tells AI systems about your business. It works like robots.txt, but instead of restricting access, it helps neural networks understand what you do. Takes about 15 minutes to create.

FAQ:
- Is llms.txt mandatory? No, it's a voluntary standard. But without it, AI systems gather information on their own and can get things wrong. The file improves accuracy and citation chances.
- How often should I update llms.txt? With every significant change: new service, updated contacts, new case studies. At minimum, review it quarterly.
- Does llms.txt replace Schema.org? No. These are different [GEO optimization](/en/blog/geo-optimizaciya-sajta-gajd/) tools. Schema.org is markup inside HTML pages. llms.txt is a separate file for AI. Best results come from using both.

### AI Search Optimization: How Neural Networks Pick Sources
**URL:** /blog/prodvizhenie-v-nejrosetyah/
**Category:** GEO
**Date:** 2026-02-22

How AI search engines like ChatGPT and Perplexity pick sources. Practical steps to get your business recommended by neural networks.

Key insight: AI search optimization (GEO) is a set of techniques that make AI engines like ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Claude recommend your business. It works through structured data on your website, not through advertising.

FAQ:
- Is AI search optimization the same as advertising? No. It's website optimization so AI systems find and quote your information organically. Like SEO, but for neural networks.
- How long does AI search optimization take? Basic steps (llms.txt, robots.txt, FAQ) take 1-2 days. Full optimization with Schema.org and case studies takes 1-2 weeks. First results appear within 2-4 weeks.
- Can I do both SEO and AI search optimization? Not only can you — you should. Many GEO elements (structure, Schema.org, E-E-A-T) also improve SEO rankings. It's not competition, it's synergy.

### How to Evaluate Google Ads Potential: An LA Case Study
**URL:** /blog/kak-ocenit-potencial-google-ads/
**Category:** MARKETING
**Date:** 2026-02-17

Case study: evaluating Google Ads potential for a permanent LED lighting installer in Los Angeles. Methodology: Google Keyword Planner API data vs industry benchmarks. Key findings: impression ceiling in LA is 1,430/month, actual CPC is 2-3x lower than top-of-page bid, Search alone yields ROAS 1-2.7x. Recommendation: multichannel (Search + LSA + YouTube + Meta) delivers ROAS 5-24x. Seasonal strategy with annual ad spend $17-27K and projected revenue $45-216K in peak quarter.
### How a Single-Page Hotel Website Loses 80,000 Searches Per Month
**URL:** /blog/odnostranichnyj-sajt-otelya-teryaet-trafik/
**Category:** SEO
**Date:** 2026-02-07

Real case study of a resort hotel: single-page website (Health Score 25/100) missing 80,000+ broad match monthly searches for 'hotel with pool' cluster, Google traffic down 38% year-over-year. Analysis of 7 semantic clusters, empty meta description, missing robots.txt/sitemap.xml/Schema.org. Prioritized fix plan — from quick wins in 1 day to creating landing pages for each keyword cluster.
### Hotel Website Audit: Rus Hotel Case Study (Sarapul, Russia)
**URL:** /blog/audit-sajta-otelya-keys-rushotel/
**Category:** MARKETING
**Date:** 2026-02-05

Case study of express audit for Rus Hotel in Sarapul, Russia: SEO analysis of two websites (scores 92 and 74), ad campaign audit in Yandex.Direct, competitor analysis of 5 hotels. Key finding — booking funnel is broken (0 completed bookings despite hundreds of ad clicks/month). Priority recommendations: fix Travelline widget, reallocate ad budget, strengthen positioning.
### Hotel Competitor Analysis: A 4-Star Property Case Study
**URL:** /blog/analiz-konkurentov-otelya-keys/
**Category:** MARKETING
**Date:** 2026-02-04

Real case study: how to analyze hotel competitors, which metrics matter, and what to do with the findings.

Key insight: Conducted competitor analysis for Grace Luxor, a 4-star hotel in Tolyatti, Russia. Found 7 competitors, compared across 8 metrics. Key finding: the hotel occupies a unique congress-hotel niche, but its 4.4 rating is below competitors — reputation management is priority #1.

FAQ:
- How often should you update competitor analysis? Basic monitoring — quarterly. Full analysis — annually or when the market changes (new competitor, rebranding).
- Which metrics matter most? Rating and number of reviews. That's what guests see when choosing a hotel. Prices change, stars don't, but reputation takes years to build.
- Do you need paid tools? For marketing analysis — no. For SEO analysis (rankings, backlinks, traffic) — yes, but that's a separate project.

### How to Prioritize Cities for Hotel SEO
**URL:** /blog/prioritizaciya-gorodov-seo-otelya/
**Category:** SEO
**Date:** 2026-02-04

How to pick the right cities for hotel SEO using traffic quality metrics. A 4-tier prioritization framework with real data.

Key insight: To prioritize cities, look beyond traffic volume. Focus on quality metrics: bounce rate, time on site, pages per session. A city with 3,000 visits and 13% bounce is more valuable than one with 15,000 visits and 25% bounce.

FAQ:
- How often should I revisit priorities? Quarterly. Seasonality affects geography: summer brings families from regional cities, winter brings corporate groups from major metros.
- What about international traffic? If bounce is under 25% and time exceeds 2 minutes — work with it. If metrics are poor — exclude from priorities but don't block the traffic.
- Do I need separate landing pages for every city? For Tier 1 — absolutely. For Tier 2 — ideally. For Tier 3 and 4 — no, better to optimize existing pages.

### GEO Optimization: How to Get Mentioned by ChatGPT and Perplexity
**URL:** /blog/chto-takoe-geo-optimizaciya/
**Category:** GEO
**Date:** 2026-02-02

What is GEO (Generative Engine Optimization), how it differs from SEO, and how to make AI systems recommend your business.

Key insight: GEO is website optimization for AI search engines (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude). Unlike SEO where links and keywords matter most, GEO focuses on data structure, FAQ sections, and llms.txt files. Early adopters get the advantage.

FAQ:
- How much does GEO optimization cost? The free Health Score provides basic diagnostics. A full GEO audit with recommendations and ready-to-use code starts at $750. This is a one-time service, not a monthly subscription.
- Will GEO replace SEO? No. These are different channels. SEO brings traffic from Google and Bing, GEO — from AI systems. Ideally, they work together.
- How fast will results appear? AI systems update data faster than search engines. After implementing changes, you'll see results in 2-4 weeks. Sometimes faster.

### How to Check Your Website's SEO: 8-Point Checklist
**URL:** /blog/kak-proverit-seo-sayta/
**Category:** SEO
**Date:** 2026-02-02

Free tools and a simple checklist for quick SEO audit. Find critical errors in 10 minutes.

Key insight: Check 8 basic parameters: Title, Description, H1, robots.txt, sitemap.xml, HTTPS, mobile version, and page speed. Takes 10 minutes and reveals critical issues. For deeper analysis, you need a full audit.

FAQ:
- Can I rank my site with just this checklist? No. This is the minimum for indexing. To grow rankings, you need content, links, and user engagement work.
- How often should I check? After every major site update. Or monthly for maintenance.
- What if I find errors? Fix by priority: robots.txt and sitemap.xml first (affect indexing), then Title and Description (affect CTR), then everything else.

### Schema.org for Local Business: Which Markup Types You Need
**URL:** /blog/schema-org-dlya-biznesa/
**Category:** SEO
**Date:** 2026-02-02

Practical guide to Schema.org for hotels, spas, clinics. Ready-to-use code examples for LocalBusiness, Hotel, FAQPage.

Key insight: Local businesses need at least three markup types: LocalBusiness (or Hotel/MedicalBusiness), FAQPage, and BreadcrumbList. This enables rich snippets in search and helps AI systems understand your business.

FAQ:
- How many markup types can I have on one page? As many as needed. A hotel homepage typically has: Hotel + FAQPage + BreadcrumbList. Just make sure each type matches the page content.
- How quickly will Google see the markup? After page indexing. Usually 1-2 weeks. You can speed it up via "Request Indexing" in Search Console.
- Do I need to hire a developer? For simple cases — no. JSON-LD can be added through CMS or manually in HTML. For complex sites with hundreds of pages, automation is better.

### SEO for Hotels: Where to Start in 2026
**URL:** /blog/seo-dlya-oteley/
**Category:** SEO
**Date:** 2026-02-01

Step-by-step guide to hotel search engine optimization. Technical SEO, local search, reputation management.

Key insight: Hotel SEO starts with technical foundation (robots.txt, sitemap.xml, Schema.org), continues with local SEO (Google Maps, Bing Places) and reputation management. First results appear in 3-6 months.

FAQ:
- How much does hotel SEO cost? From $1,500/month for comprehensive promotion. Price depends on property size, market competition, and service scope.
- How soon will results appear? First rankings in 3-6 months. Local SEO shows results faster — from 1-2 months.
- Can I do without SEO? Yes, but it's expensive. OTAs take 15-25% from each booking. SEO is an investment in reducing dependence on intermediaries.

**Blog page:** https://vlasdobry.ru/blog/
